question_title: How many deaths will be attributed to the 2026 Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in Ituri Province, DRC, as reported by Africa CDC by May 31, 2026?
summary: This forecast covers cumulative deaths from the 2026 Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in Ituri Province, DRC, as Africa CDC will report by May 31, 2026. As of May 21, US CDC reports 148 suspected deaths with 575 suspected and 51 confirmed cases. Deaths have grown from 80 (May 15) to 148 (May 21), roughly 11 deaths/day and accelerating with cases doubling every ~5 days. Key drivers: no vaccine for Bundibugyo species, operational constraints (insecurity, population mobility, limited testing), and spread to multiple provinces including North Kivu/Goma. Moderating factors: massive international response (PHEIC/PHECS declared), WHO/MSF/IFRC deploying. Monte Carlo simulation with scenario-weighted growth rates (18% rapid deceleration, 35% moderate, 22% sustained, 11% rapid growth, 7% explosive, plus 7% chance of limited Africa CDC reporting) gives median ~287 deaths. Distribution is right-skewed reflecting outbreak upside risk. Lower bound scenarios include Africa CDC not updating beyond May 18 (~106 deaths, ~2% probability) or single additional update with ~May 22 data (~170 deaths, ~5%).

factors.description: Deaths already at 148 as of May 21 (US CDC), with 10 days until resolution deadline. High floor established.
Rapid case growth: 246 to 600+ suspected cases in 5 days (May 15-20). 103% surge in 96 hours. Exponential growth phase.
No vaccine for Bundibugyo species. Response limited to supportive care, contact tracing, safe burials.
Operational constraints: insecurity, mining-related mobility, limited testing, PPE shortages, weeks of undetected transmission before declaration.
Massive international response: PHEIC declared May 16, PHECS May 18. WHO deploying 35+ experts, 11.5t supplies. IFRC CHF 29M appeal.
Resolution depends on Africa CDC's reported figure, which may lag actual deaths by 2-3 days. But question uses 'more inclusive' figure.
Spread to North Kivu (including Goma, 1M+ pop) and cross-border to Uganda. Risk of much larger outbreak if urban transmission established.
Bundibugyo CFR historically 25-50%. Current crude CFR ~25%. With 600+ cases, deaths will continue accumulating from existing and new cases.

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary: Ebola Bundibugyo Deaths by May 31, 2026

**Median estimate: 287 deaths** (80% CI: 209–449)

### Key Data Points
| Date | Source | Deaths | Cases |
|------|--------|--------|-------|
| May 15 | Africa CDC | 65 | 246 suspected |
| May 15 | WHO DON | 80 | 246 suspected |
| May 18 | Africa CDC PHECS | ~106 | ~395 suspected |
| May 19 | CARE/MSF | 134 | 536 suspected |
| May 20 | WHO DG briefing | 139 | ~600 suspected |
| May 21 | US CDC | 148 | 575 suspected, 51 confirmed |

### Key Drivers
- **Upward:** No vaccine for Bundibugyo, rapid case growth (~doubling every 5 days), operational constraints (insecurity, mobility, limited testing), spread to multiple provinces
- **Downward:** Massive international response (PHEIC/PHECS), WHO/MSF/IFRC deploying substantial resources
- **Uncertainty:** Africa CDC reporting frequency and lag, whether urban transmission in Goma establishes, and how quickly response interventions slow transmission

### Distribution Shape
Right-skewed with a small left tail reflecting the ~2% chance Africa CDC doesn't update beyond May 18 (resolving at ~106). The upper tail extends above 500 (~6%) reflecting risk of explosive urban spread.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) How many deaths will be attributed to the 2026 Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in Ituri Province, DRC, as reported by Africa CDC by May 31, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43565)
[Ebola virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda](https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/ebola-virus-disease-outbreak-democratic-republic-congo-and-uganda-19-may-2026)
[Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda](https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2026-DON602)

[Africa CDC Declares the Ongoing Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak a Public Health Emergency of Continental Security – Africa CDC](https://africacdc.org/news-item/africa-cdc-declares-the-ongoing-bundibugyo-ebola-outbreak-a-public-health-emergency-of-continental-security/)

[EBOLA BUNDIBUGYO VIRUS DISEASE OUTBREAK Democratic Republic of the Congo | Uganda Weekly External Situation Report 01, Data as of 18 May 2026](https://www.afro.who.int/countries/democratic-republic-of-congo/publication/ebola-bundibugyo-virus-disease-outbreak-democratic-republic-congo-uganda-weekly-external-situation)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the total cumulative number of deaths (combining laboratory-confirmed, probable, and suspected/community deaths) attributed to the 2026 Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in Ituri Province, DRC, as reported by Africa CDC in its most recent public update (press release, situation report, or Epidemic Intelligence Weekly Report) published on or before May 31, 2026. The Africa CDC Ebola page (https://africacdc.org/disease/ebola/) and Africa CDC news page (https://africacdc.org/news/) will be consulted to identify the latest figure. Report the figure as an integer count of deaths (e.g., a reported total of 112 deaths resolves as 112). If Africa CDC reports a numeric range, the midpoint rounded to the nearest integer is used. If Africa CDC reports both a narrower 'deaths among reported cases' figure and a separate broader 'community deaths' or 'suspected deaths' figure for this same outbreak, the higher (more inclusive) figure is used. If Africa CDC has not published any update beyond the 15 May 2026 statement, the figure of 65 from that statement will be used.
fine_print: Only deaths occurring inside DRC and attributed to the Ituri Bundibugyo outbreak count; the imported case death in Uganda and any other extraterritorial deaths are excluded. If Africa CDC publishes multiple updates by May 31, 2026, the one with the most recent publication date is used; if multiple updates share the same publication date, the one with the larger reported death total is used. If Africa CDC's pages are inaccessible at resolution time, the equivalent figure from the WHO Disease Outbreak News page (https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news) for this outbreak will be used as a fallback, or, failing that, figures from [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).

factors.description: Deaths already at 148 as of May 21 (US CDC), with 10 days until resolution deadline. Floor is high.
Rapid case growth: 246→600+ suspected cases in 5 days (May 15-20). 103% surge in 96 hours. Exponential growth phase.
No vaccine for Bundibugyo species. Response limited to supportive care, contact tracing, safe burials. Major constraint.
Operational constraints: insecurity, mining-related mobility, limited testing, PPE shortages, weeks of undetected transmission before declaration.
Massive international response: PHEIC declared May 16, PHECS May 18. WHO deploying 35+ experts, 11.5t supplies. IFRC CHF 29M appeal. US CDC mobilized.
Resolution depends on Africa CDC's reported figure, which may lag actual deaths by 2-3 days. But question uses 'more inclusive' figure.
Spread to North Kivu (including Goma, 1M+ pop), Kinshasa, and cross-border to Uganda. Risk of much larger outbreak.
Bundibugyo CFR historically 25-50%. Current crude CFR ~25%. With 600+ cases, expected cumulative deaths from known cases alone ~150-200.

input.anchor: Deaths as of May 21: 148. With 10 days remaining and ~10-14 deaths/day accruing (accelerating), linear extrapolation gives ~248-288. Epidemic growth could push this higher.
input.assessment: This is a rapidly growing Ebola outbreak with no vaccine available. Deaths have grown from 80 (May 15) to 148 (May 21) - roughly 11 deaths/day. The outbreak is in exponential growth phase with cases doubling every ~5 days. Key factors pushing deaths higher: no vaccine, operational constraints, spread to multiple provinces. Factors moderating: massive international response (PHEIC), contact tracing scaling up. The resolution depends on what Africa CDC reports by May 31, which adds uncertainty about whether they'll have the latest data. Monte Carlo simulation using scenario-weighted growth rates gives median ~287 deaths with wide tails reflecting genuine uncertainty about epidemic trajectory.
input.calibration_notes: The simulation P10=209, P90=449 represents about a 2x range. This seems appropriate given: (1) confirmed rapid growth, (2) but response scaling, (3) uncertainty in Africa CDC reporting cadence. The lower tail includes a 7% chance Africa CDC doesn't update beyond May 18-22, giving figures of 106-190. Upper tail reflects possibility of sustained exponential growth.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. How quickly will response interventions (contact tracing, isolation, safe burials) slow transmission? 2. Will the outbreak establish sustained transmission in Goma or other major cities? 3. How frequently will Africa CDC update their figures? 4. How complete is the current community death count?
input.tool_audit: Useful: research() sub-agent found excellent data from ReliefWeb. web_search found Wikipedia, ECDC, US CDC with latest figures. fetch_url extracted detailed WHO DON report. Empty/limited: Africa CDC website pages didn't show latest outbreak data directly. Failures: None.
input.update_triggers: 1. Africa CDC publishes new situation report with updated death count. 2. Confirmed sustained transmission in Goma or Kinshasa. 3. Announcement of experimental vaccine/therapeutic trial. 4. Reports of dramatic slowing in new case counts.
input.process_reflection: The research sub-agent was very effective at finding multiple data points across sources. The main challenge was reconciling different death figures from different sources on similar dates. The simulation approach worked well for capturing epidemic dynamics uncertainty. The scenario-weighted model is more appropriate than a single growth rate model given the high structural uncertainty about response effectiveness.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
**Verification Summary**

I checked all 8 factors against the research trace, verified resolution criteria alignment, checked for double-counting, wrong-direction factors, and worldview consistency.

**Factor 1 (148 deaths as of May 21, US CDC):** ✅ Verified. The trace shows the US CDC situation summary page explicitly states "As of May 21... 148 suspected deaths" (trace line 712).

**Factor 2 (Rapid case growth 246→600+, 103% surge):** ✅ Verified. IRC reported 246→500 in 96 hours (103% increase); Project HOPE reported 600+ as of May 20. The factor slightly conflates two data points (5-day span vs 96-hour span) but the core claim is well-supported.

**Factor 3 (No vaccine for Bundibugyo):** ✅ Verified. WHO DON confirms "no approved vaccines or specific treatments currently exist for BVD."

**Factor 4 (Operational constraints):** ✅ Verified. Multiple trace sources confirm insecurity, mining-related mobility, limited testing, PPE shortages, weeks of undetected transmission.

**Factor 5 (International response, logit=-0.8):** ✅ Verified. PHEIC May 16, PHECS May 18, WHO 35+ experts, 11.5t supplies, IFRC CHF 29M. Direction correct (pushes estimate down).

**Factor 6 (Reporting lag, logit=-0.3):** ✅ Verified. Trace shows Africa CDC's May 15 figure (65) lagged WHO's same-date figure (80). Direction correct.

**Factor 7 (Spread to North Kivu, Kinshasa, Uganda):** ⚠️ Minor inaccuracy. The trace shows the Kinshasa case actually tested NEGATIVE per WHO DON ("tested NEGATIVE for Bundibugyo virus on confirmatory testing"), yet the factor claims spread to Kinshasa. However, Wikipedia does reference "imported cases" in Kinshasa (possibly pending cases or different ones). North Kivu/Goma spread and Uganda cross-border are well-verified. Factor confidence is appropriately low (0.5) and this is not a dominant driver.

**Factor 8 (Bundibugyo CFR 25-50%):** ✅ Verified. Historical range from 2007 (25%) and 2012 (51%) outbreaks. Current crude CFR ~25% matches trace data (148/575).

**Resolution criteria alignment:** ✅ Good. The agent correctly identifies that resolution depends on Africa CDC's reported figure (not actual deaths), accounts for reporting lag, correctly notes the May 18 PHECS update supersedes the May 15 fallback figure, and uses the "more inclusive" figure approach per criteria.

**Double-counting:** None found. Factors address distinct aspects: current data, growth rate, vaccine availability, operational constraints, response, reporting mechanism, geographic spread, and CFR.

**Wrong-direction factors:** None. Factors 5 and 6 correctly have negative logits (pushing estimate down).

**Probability assessment:** The center of 287 with range 209-449 is well-supported by: (a) linear extrapolation from 148 at ~10-14 deaths/day giving ~248-288; (b) the Monte Carlo simulation accounting for growth deceleration; (c) the question's 50-500 bounds. The estimate is reasonable.

**Counterargument quality:** ✅ Strong. The agent constructs a coherent alternative case around daily death accrual plateauing, reporting lag reducing Africa CDC's figure, and rapid response effectiveness. It specifies concrete trigger conditions for mind-changing.

**Confidence (0.35):** ✅ Appropriately humble for a 10-day epidemic projection with high genuine uncertainty.

**Tool audit consistency:** ✅ Agent claims "no failures" which matches the 0% error rate in tool metrics.

**Worldview consistency:** No contradictions found. Worldview research entries either lacked current data (tools unavailable) or were superseded.

The only issue found is the minor Kinshasa spread claim in Factor 7, where the WHO DON says the case tested negative. This does not materially affect the forecast — the factor has low confidence (0.5), moderate weight (logit +0.8), and its core claims about North Kivu/Goma and Uganda are well-verified. Removing the Kinshasa claim would not meaningfully change the estimate.
